questions.question: What is the core question the Superalignment agenda asks?
options: Can RLHF scale beyond 1B parameters?
Can adversarial training remove all backdoors?
Can a weaker overseer reliably supervise a stronger, aligned model?
Are humans more accurate than reward models?

questions.question: In Burns et al. (2023) weak-to-strong generalization, what is the setup?
options: A reward model is trained on synthetic data only
Two strong models debate; a human judges
GPT-2 is fine-tuned on GPT-4 labels
A strong model (GPT-4 class) is fine-tuned on labels produced by a weak model (GPT-2 class) and its capability is measured against the strong supervised ceiling

questions.question: How is Performance Gap Recovered (PGR) defined?
options: (ceiling - fine-tuned) / weak
(fine-tuned - weak) / (ceiling - weak)
fine-tuned / ceiling
(weak - ceiling) / ceiling

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a scalable-oversight mechanism listed in the lesson?
options: Task Decomposition
Tokenizer Distillation
Recursive Reward Modeling
Debate

questions.question: How are scalable oversight and W2SG complementary?
options: Scalable oversight improves the overseer's effective signal quality; W2SG closes the gap from whatever imperfect signal the overseer provides
They both target the same metric
They are mutually exclusive
They both require white-box access

questions.question: What is the central assumption of the AI-safety-via-debate mechanism?
options: Debate transcripts are always shorter than direct answers
Finding a convincing true answer is easier than finding a convincing false answer
The judge is more capable than the debaters
Both debaters always tell the truth
